dimensions.name: root_cause_correctness
dimensions.note: Root cause correctly identified: iterative BA refinement updated Technical Hints to the final M5 regex but left the Edge Cases 'Critical regression risk' bullet on the iteration-2 form. Verified by direct read of ticket-20260526-052559.md:120 (stale form confirmed present) and :197 (authoritative form confirmed present). Hook _BULK_CANONICAL_RE assignment confirmed at hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:677. The 'why introduced' (iterative drafting left one section un-synchronized) and 'why problematic' (reader sees a form that admits --sid FORGED suffixes) are both accurately stated.

dimensions.name: scope_correctness
dimensions.note: Scope is correctly bounded to exactly one file (docs/dev/ticket-20260526-052559.md), one line change. Non-goals are explicit and complete: Technical Hints (already correct), hook files, tests, scripts, other sections. The IS_SUBAGENT gate task is correctly excluded from scope — per the brief it was dropped as future hardening in close-report-20260526-052559.md and must not be flagged as missing scope. BA correctly omits it without mention.

dimensions.name: ac_completeness
dimensions.note: AC1's check.must_be_absent field contains a broken grep pattern: "source\\[\\[:space:\\]\\]\\+venv/bin/activate.*optional-double-venv-group". The suffix 'optional-double-venv-group' is a human-readable label, not a regex fragment that matches the actual stale string. A mechanical executor running 'grep -E' or 'grep -F' with this pattern against the file will find nothing and incorrectly conclude the stale form is absent — a false-pass risk. The sibling 'stale_string' field in the same check object contains the correct literal stale regex, but 'must_be_absent' is the semantically designated field for the absence check and it is unusable as written. This is a spec_text_vs_execution_drift defect: the literal check recipe diverges from the intent. Fix required: replace the must_be_absent value with the exact stale regex string (the same value already in the stale_string field), or remove the must_be_absent field and rely solely on stale_string for the absence check. AC2, AC3, and AC4 are well-formed and mechanically testable.

dimensions.name: artifact_consistency
dimensions.note: Ticket, context JSON, and AC JSON are internally consistent on scope, the stale regex value, the authoritative regex value, the target location (Edge Cases section, Critical regression risk bullet, line ~120 advisory), and the reference source. Minor cosmetic inaccuracy: BA cites 'hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:678' as the location of _BULK_CANONICAL_RE throughout all three artifacts. Direct grep confirms the _BULK_CANONICAL_RE assignment is on line 677; line 678 is the 'if [[ $COMMAND =~ $_BULK_CANONICAL_RE ]]; then' statement. The regex value cited is correct; only the line number is off by one. This does not affect correctness and is non-blocking.

dimensions.name: risk_coverage
dimensions.note: All relevant risks for a MICRO doc-only fix are covered: (1) single-occurrence guarantee with pause-and-report instruction if stale form appears more than once; (2) explicit do-not-modify-Technical-Hints guard (line 197 already correct); (3) hook-file is reference-only with explicit prohibition on hook modification and a pause-and-report condition if the hook no longer matches the authoritative regex at verification time. No unaddressed risks for this scope. The Codex consultation (context JSON codex_consult) is recorded as invoked with 5 findings applied, all scope-tightening — this was appropriate and improved AC precision.

blocking_issues.description: AC1.check.must_be_absent contains a non-functional grep pattern: "source\\[\\[:space:\\]\\]\\+venv/bin/activate.*optional-double-venv-group". The fragment 'optional-double-venv-group' is a human-readable label, not a regex that matches the stale string. A QA or dev agent running this as a grep will get zero matches and falsely conclude the stale form is absent. This is a spec_text_vs_execution_drift defect: the literal verification recipe in the field diverges from the intent (which is: verify the stale regex string is gone).
blocking_issues.required_fix: Replace AC1.check.must_be_absent with the exact stale regex literal that is already present in the sibling AC1.check.stale_string field: "source[[:space:]]+venv/bin/activate[[:space:]]*&&[[:space:]]*(source[[:space:]]+venv/bin/activate[[:space:]]*&&[[:space:]]*)?python3?[[:space:]]+/root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel\\.py". Alternatively, remove must_be_absent and document that stale_string is the canonical absent-check string. Either approach makes the check mechanically executable.

non_blocking_observations.location: ticket-dev-20260526-203808.md lines 33-34, 48-49; context-dev-20260526-203808.json root_cause_analysis.observations[2] and component_chain[1]; reference_source.url_or_location
non_blocking_observations.description: All three artifacts cite 'hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:678' as the location of _BULK_CANONICAL_RE. Direct grep confirms the _BULK_CANONICAL_RE assignment ('_BULK_CANONICAL_RE=...') is on line 677; line 678 is the 'if [[ $COMMAND =~ $_BULK_CANONICAL_RE ]]; then' statement. The regex value cited is correct; only the line attribution is off by one. Non-blocking — dev can locate the correct line trivially.
